title: RH-EML: Analytic Continuation & Functional Equation

functional_eq.object: Functional equation ξ(s) = ξ(1-s): completed zeta
functional_eq.why: Γ(s/2): analytic continuation via exp(i·Im(s)·log) = EML-3

symmetry.implication: Functional equation is DEPTH-SYMMETRIC about Re=1/2

ring_interpretation.symmetry: ξ(s) = ξ(1-s): EML-3 = EML-3 ✓ (depth preserved by functional equation)

gamma.object: Gamma function Γ(s) in functional equation
gamma.why: Γ(s) = ∫₀^∞ t^{s-1}e^{-t}dt: for complex s, Γ(1/2+it) = EML-3

two_level.object: Two-level ring interpretation of functional equation

real_part.why: σ = Re(s): real parameter, measurement = EML-2

insight.ring_action: σ(EML-2) ↔ 1-σ(EML-2): measurement domain symmetric; t(EML-3) ↔ -t(EML-3): oscillatory domain symmetric
insight.verdict: Functional equation = two-level ring symmetry: (EML-2 domain)×(EML-3 oscillation) ✓

verdicts.two_level_FE: Functional equation = two-level ring symmetry in (σ,t) plane
verdicts.new_insight: Re(s)=EML-2, Im(s)=EML-3: functional equation preserves both strata

key_theorem: Functional Equation Depth Theorem (S319): Completed zeta ξ(s) = EML-3 (Gamma×Zeta: 3⊗3=3). Functional equation ξ(s)=ξ(1-s) is DEPTH-SYMMETRIC about Re=1/2. NEW: Functional equation = two-level ring symmetry: Real part σ (EML-2, measurement) maps to 1-σ (EML-2); Imaginary part t (EML-3, oscillation) maps to -t (EML-3). Both strata are independently preserved by the functional equation. This implies: RH (Re(s)=1/2) is where EML-2 and EML-3 strata are in EQUILIBRIUM.
rabbit_hole_log: NEW: σ=EML-2 (measurement), t=EML-3 (oscillation): two-level structure in (σ,t)
RH = equilibrium line where EML-2 and EML-3 strata balance
